ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Full name of the state (e.g., Alabama, Alaska) */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* Abbreviated state code (e.g., AL, AK) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the state belongs to (e.g., Northeast, South) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "enroll" INTEGER /* Total student enrollment in the state */,
  "total_revenue" REAL /* Total revenue for education in the state */,
  "federal_revenue" INTEGER /* Federal revenue for education in the state */,
  "state_revenue" REAL /* State revenue for education in the state */,
  "avg_math_4_score" REAL /* Average math score for 4th graders */,
  "avg_math_8_score" REAL /* Average math score for 8th graders */,
  "avg_reading_4_score" REAL /* Average reading score for 4th graders */,
  "avg_reading_8_score" REAL /* Average reading score for 8th graders */,
  "fed_per_student" REAL /* Federal revenue per student */,
  "state_per_student" REAL /* State revenue per student */,
  "total_per_student" REAL /* Total revenue per student */,
  "pct_in_poverty" REAL /* Percentage of population in poverty */,
  "pct_with_ba" REAL /* Percentage of population with a bachelor's degree */,
  "graduation_rate" REAL /* High school graduation rate */,
  "dropout_rate" REAL /* High school dropout rate */,
  "avg_teacher_salary" REAL /* Average teacher salary in the state */,
  "student_teacher_ratio" REAL /* Average student-teacher ratio in the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_data_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_expenditures" (
  "expenditure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each expenditure entry */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the expenditure belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the expenditure was made */,
  "instruction_expenditure" REAL /* Expenditure on instruction */,
  "support_services_expenditure" REAL /* Expenditure on support services */,
  "other_expenditure" REAL /* Other expenditures */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expenditure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_expenditures_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* User's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity performed (e.g., login, data access) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the request */,
  "request_type" TEXT /* Type of data requested (e.g., state data, national data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_exports" (
  "export_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each export */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the export */,
  "export_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the export */,
  "export_format" TEXT /* Format of the exported data (e.g., CSV, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("export_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_exports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
